evidence_quotes.quote: Right now the area is unclear and messy, where video game vendors can remove access to goods you have paid for unlike any other industry or goods.

We need to unify an "End of life plan" (Like in the EU citizens iniative: Stop Destroying Video Games), or similar requirement for video game vendors to guarantee some sort of access to your purchases.

paragraphs.text: I think it would be important to add protections to customers who have bought digital goods, namely video games. Right now the area is unclear and messy, where video game vendors can remove access to goods you have paid for unlike any other industry or goods. While the directive about it says that digital goods should always be refunded when access ends (which is also unreasonable), we need to unify an "End of life plan" (Like in the EU citizens iniative: Stop Destroying Video Games), or similar requirement for video game vendors to guarantee some sort of access to your purchases. We need to keep internet free, pushing for digital ID to be used on content the EU views as "problematic" is a slippery slope that can lead to eroding the freedoms and rights of citizens and democracy itself. We need some sort of assurances that the completely free access to internet is protected for all EU citizens now and forever.
